evals.prompt: I need to add a streak tracking feature to my Flutter app called ThinkDaily. Write the full stack: a StreakRepository (abstract interface + implementation), a Riverpod provider that reads from it, and a StreakWidget that displays the current streak count with loading and error states. The app uses flutter_riverpod with code generation (@riverpod annotation), GoRouter for navigation, and freezed for models. Save all output Dart files to the outputs/ directory.
evals.expected_output: Clean architecture code: abstract StreakRepository interface, implementation class, @riverpod annotated provider using AsyncValue, ConsumerWidget using .when() for all three states, no setState, no Navigator.push, no hardcoded colors.

Refactored code with: ConsumerWidget, @riverpod provider, repository pattern (no direct Dio in widget), AsyncValue.when() for all states, context.go() instead of Navigator.push, AppColors/AppTextStyles instead of hardcoded values.

evals.prompt: Create a UserProfile screen for ThinkDaily that shows: display name, total problems solved, current streak, and accuracy percentage. Wire it up completely — Riverpod provider using @riverpod annotation, a UserStatsRepository interface, and the screen widget using ConsumerWidget. Assume no real backend yet — use hardcoded data in the repository impl for now. Error and loading states must be handled. Save all files to outputs/.
evals.expected_output: Full feature: abstract UserStatsRepository, UserStatsRepositoryImpl with mock data, @riverpod provider with AsyncValue, ConsumerWidget using .when() with error/loading/data states, const constructors, no hardcoded colors or text styles.
